questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Left turns are not allowed
U-turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What should you do at a steady red traffic light?
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the light turns green
Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light always means come to a full stop and wait until the signal turns green before proceeding.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic signal means you should:
choices: Stop, then proceed when it is safe, as at a stop sign
Drive through without slowing
Treat it as a green light
Stop and wait for the light to turn green
questions.explanation: A flashing red light is treated exactly like a stop sign: stop completely, then go when it is safe.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light indicate?
choices: The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
Speed up to beat the red light
The intersection is closed
Pedestrians have the right-of-way only
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that red is coming, so you should stop if you can do so safely rather than racing through.

questions.prompt: An eight-sided (octagon) traffic sign always means:
choices: Stop
Yield
Do not enter
Speed limit ahead
questions.explanation: The octagon shape is reserved exclusively for stop signs so drivers can recognize them even when words are not visible.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow line on your side of the center of the road means:
choices: Passing is permitted when it is safe
Passing is never allowed
The road is one-way
You must stop ahead
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line indicates that passing is allowed from your side when the way ahead is clear and safe.

questions.prompt: What color and meaning do construction and maintenance zone signs typically have?
choices: Orange, warning of road work ahead
Blue, indicating services
Green, giving directions
Brown, marking recreation areas
questions.explanation: Orange signs identify construction and maintenance areas where you should slow down and watch for workers.

questions.prompt: A flashing yellow traffic light means:
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Come to a complete stop
The signal is broken and should be ignored
Turn around and find another route
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light tells you to slow down and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: A solid white line between lanes traveling the same direction means:
choices: Stay in your lane; changing lanes is discouraged
Passing is encouraged
The lane ahead ends
You must exit the highway
questions.explanation: A solid white line discourages crossing, so you should remain in your lane where one is marked.

questions.prompt: What does a red, triangular sign pointing downward mean?
choices: Yield to other traffic
Stop completely
No entry
Construction zone
questions.explanation: The downward-pointing triangle is the yield sign, meaning slow down and give the right-of-way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: When a traffic signal is completely dark due to a power failure, you should treat the intersection as:
choices: An all-way stop
A green light in all directions
A yield for the larger road only
Closed to all traffic
questions.explanation: A dark signal should be treated as an all-way stop, so each driver stops and proceeds in turn.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, if two vehicles arrive at the same time, who has the right-of-way?
choices: The driver on the right
The driver on the left
The faster vehicle
The larger vehicle
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: In Montana, when may you turn right at a red light?
choices: After a complete stop and yielding, unless a sign prohibits it
Never
Only when an arrow is shown
Without stopping if no one is around
questions.explanation: Right turns on red are allowed after a full stop and yielding to pedestrians and cross traffic, unless a posted sign forbids it.

questions.prompt: When you want to turn left at an intersection, you must:
choices: Yield to oncoming traffic that is close enough to be a hazard
Proceed first because left turns have priority
Honk and turn immediately
Wait for a red light before turning
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles that are close enough to pose a danger before completing the turn.

questions.prompt: A pedestrian is crossing in a marked crosswalk ahead of you. You should:
choices: Stop and yield to the pedestrian
Sound your horn so they hurry
Continue if you can pass behind them
Speed up to clear the crosswalk first
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians in a crosswalk and let them finish crossing safely.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren, you should:
choices: Pull over to the right and stop until it passes
Speed up to stay ahead of it
Stop immediately in your lane
Continue at the same speed
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right edge of the road and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, you should:
choices: Yield to traffic already in the roundabout
Stop in the circle to let others in
Enter quickly before checking traffic
Always come to a full stop before entering
questions.explanation: Traffic already circulating in a roundabout has the right-of-way, so you enter only when there is a safe gap.

questions.prompt: When making a turn, you should signal at least:
choices: 100 feet before the turn
10 feet before the turn
Only after you begin turning
300 feet before the turn
questions.explanation: Signaling at least 100 feet ahead gives other drivers time to react to your intended turn.

questions.prompt: At an uncontrolled intersection where two roads meet and there are no signs or signals, you should:
choices: Yield to the vehicle that arrived first or is on your right
Always go first
Speed through quickly
Stop and wait for a police officer
questions.explanation: At an uncontrolled intersection, yield to the vehicle that got there first, or to the one on your right if you arrive together.

questions.prompt: When you are about to enter a road from a driveway or parking lot, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic and pedestrians already on the road
Force your way into traffic
Assume other drivers will stop for you
Enter without looking if you are in a hurry
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering a roadway must yield to traffic and pedestrians already using it.

questions.prompt: When two vehicles approach a narrow mountain road and cannot pass, which vehicle generally has the right-of-way?
choices: The vehicle going uphill
The vehicle going downhill
The larger vehicle
Whichever honks first
questions.explanation: On a steep, narrow road the vehicle traveling downhill should yield and back up, because the uphill vehicle has less control when backing.

questions.prompt: What is the default speed limit in a Montana urban district unless otherwise posted?
choices: 25 mph
15 mph
35 mph
45 mph
questions.explanation: Montana law sets the default urban district speed limit at 25 mph unless signs show otherwise.

questions.prompt: On a Montana interstate highway outside an urbanized area of 50,000 or more, the daytime speed limit is:
choices: 80 mph
65 mph
70 mph
75 mph
questions.explanation: Montana's statutory interstate limit outside large urbanized areas is 80 mph.

questions.prompt: The 'basic speed rule' requires drivers to:
choices: Drive no faster than is reasonable and prudent for conditions
Always drive exactly at the posted limit
Match the speed of surrounding traffic only
Drive 5 mph below the limit at all times
questions.explanation: The basic speed rule means you must adjust to conditions and may need to drive below the posted limit when it is unsafe.

questions.prompt: A good rule for safe following distance in normal conditions is the:
choices: Three-second rule
One-second rule
Half-second rule
Ten-car-length rule at any speed
questions.explanation: The three-second rule helps you keep enough space to react and stop safely behind the vehicle ahead.

questions.prompt: On most Montana public highways that are not interstates, the daytime speed limit is:
choices: 70 mph
55 mph
60 mph
80 mph
questions.explanation: On other public highways in Montana the statutory daytime limit is 70 mph, dropping to 65 mph at night.

questions.prompt: You should increase your following distance when:
choices: Roads are wet, icy, or visibility is poor
Traffic is light
You are in a hurry
The road is dry and straight
questions.explanation: Poor weather and reduced visibility lengthen stopping distances, so you need extra space ahead.

questions.prompt: If a vehicle is following you too closely (tailgating), you should:
choices: Increase your following distance from the vehicle ahead
Brake suddenly to warn them
Speed up to lose them
Move to the shoulder and stop
questions.explanation: Leaving more room ahead gives you time to slow gradually, reducing the chance the tailgater hits you.

questions.prompt: Within a Montana interstate inside an urbanized area of 50,000 or more, the speed limit is:
choices: 65 mph
80 mph
55 mph
45 mph
questions.explanation: Montana lowers the interstate limit to 65 mph within large urbanized areas.

questions.prompt: Driving too slowly for conditions is dangerous because it can:
choices: Block traffic flow and cause others to make risky maneuvers
Always be the safest choice
Improve fuel economy in every case
Never affect other drivers
questions.explanation: Driving far below the flow of traffic can frustrate other drivers and lead to unsafe passing.

questions.prompt: You should not pass another vehicle when:
choices: A solid yellow line is on your side of the road
A broken yellow line is on your side
You are on a multi-lane highway
The road ahead is clear and straight
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side prohibits passing because of limited sight distance or other hazards.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, slower traffic should generally:
choices: Keep to the right and use the left lane only to pass
Stay in the left lane
Drive in the center lane only
Change lanes frequently
questions.explanation: Keeping right except to pass allows faster traffic to flow safely in the left lane.

questions.prompt: When another vehicle is passing you, you should:
choices: Maintain or slightly reduce your speed
Speed up to prevent the pass
Move into the other lane
Brake hard immediately
questions.explanation: Never speed up while being passed; holding or easing your speed lets the other driver complete the pass safely.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should always:
choices: Signal and check mirrors and your blind spot
Just glance in the mirror
Rely on your backup camera only
Change quickly without signaling
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking mirrors plus your blind spot ensures no vehicle is hidden beside you before you move over.

questions.prompt: It is unsafe and illegal to pass when:
choices: Approaching a hill or curve where you cannot see far enough ahead
On a straight road with a broken center line
On a clearly marked passing zone
Traffic ahead is far away and visible
questions.explanation: Passing near hills and curves is prohibited because oncoming vehicles may be hidden from view.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, you should return to your lane when:
choices: You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
Your front bumper is even with it
You are halfway past it
You feel like it, at any time
questions.explanation: Seeing the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror means you have enough room to return to the lane safely.

questions.prompt: You may not pass another vehicle:
choices: Within an intersection or railroad crossing
On a dry, open highway
In a marked passing zone
When traffic ahead is clear
questions.explanation: Passing is prohibited at intersections and railroad crossings because of the higher risk of conflict there.

questions.prompt: When driving in heavy traffic, the safest lane discipline is to:
choices: Pick a lane and avoid weaving between lanes
Change lanes constantly to gain position
Straddle two lanes
Drive on the shoulder to pass
questions.explanation: Staying in one lane and avoiding unnecessary lane changes keeps traffic predictable and reduces collisions.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a motorcycle, you should:
choices: Give it a full lane width and extra following distance
Share the lane beside it
Follow it closely
Pass it without signaling
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are entitled to a full lane and can stop quickly, so give them room and more following distance.

questions.prompt: Montana's Move Over law requires drivers approaching a stopped emergency or work vehicle with warning lights to:
choices: Slow down and move over a lane when possible
Maintain speed and stay in the lane
Speed up to clear the area
Stop completely in the travel lane
questions.explanation: The Move Over law protects roadside workers by requiring you to slow down and change lanes away from them when you safely can.

questions.prompt: Large trucks have large blind spots, so you should:
choices: Avoid lingering alongside them where the driver cannot see you
Stay close behind them at all times
Pass on the right whenever possible
Cut in front right after passing
questions.explanation: If you cannot see the truck driver's mirrors, the driver cannot see you, so do not linger in those blind spots.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist on the road, you should:
choices: Give a safe clearance and wait for a clear gap
Pass closely to save time
Honk to make them move over
Expect them to ride on the sidewalk
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have a right to the road, so pass with ample space and only when it is safe to do so.

questions.prompt: A large truck signals a right turn but swings wide to the left first. You should:
choices: Not try to squeeze past on the right
Pass quickly on the right
Honk and pull alongside
Assume the driver is changing lanes left
questions.explanation: Trucks need extra room for wide right turns, so never try to pass on the right when a truck is turning.

questions.prompt: On rural Montana roads you should watch especially for:
choices: Slow-moving farm equipment and wildlife
Only other passenger cars
City buses
Toll booths
questions.explanation: Rural roads often have slow farm machinery and crossing wildlife, especially at dawn and dusk.

questions.prompt: A vehicle displaying a slow-moving vehicle emblem (orange triangle) means:
choices: The vehicle travels at 25 mph or less
The vehicle is an emergency vehicle
You must stop behind it
The vehicle is broken down
questions.explanation: The orange triangle warns that the vehicle moves slowly, so approach with caution and be ready to slow down.

questions.prompt: When you see a pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog, you must:
choices: Yield the right-of-way; the pedestrian may be visually impaired
Honk to alert them
Proceed normally
Drive around quickly
questions.explanation: A white cane or guide dog signals a visually impaired pedestrian who always has the right-of-way.

questions.prompt: In Montana, the legal BAC limit for a driver age 21 or older is:
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and older are considered legally impaired at a BAC of 0.08 or higher.

questions.prompt: For drivers under 21 in Montana, the zero-tolerance BAC limit is:
choices: 0.00 with no measurable amount allowed
questions.explanation: Montana's zero-tolerance law makes it illegal for drivers under 21 to drive with a BAC of 0.02 or higher.

questions.prompt: Under Montana's implied consent law, refusing a chemical test when lawfully arrested for DUI results in:
choices: An automatic license suspension
A warning only
No consequences
A reduced fine
questions.explanation: By driving you consent to chemical testing, and refusing one leads to an automatic suspension of your license.

questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes distracted driving?
choices: Any activity that takes your eyes, hands, or mind off driving
Only texting
Only eating
Only talking to passengers
questions.explanation: Distraction is anything visual, manual, or mental that pulls your attention away from the driving task.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect your ability to drive?
choices: It slows reaction time and impairs judgment and coordination
It improves concentration
It sharpens reflexes
It has no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reactions and degrades judgment and coordination, all of which make driving dangerous.

questions.prompt: The safest way to handle your phone for navigation is to:
choices: Set the route before you start driving
Type the address while driving
Hold the phone in your hand
Glance at it frequently at speed
questions.explanation: Setting navigation before you drive keeps your attention on the road instead of your phone.

questions.prompt: Prescription or over-the-counter medications can:
choices: Impair your driving even if legally obtained
Never affect driving ability
Only matter for commercial drivers
Improve your driving
questions.explanation: Many legal medications cause drowsiness or impairment, so always check labels before driving.

questions.prompt: The only effective way to lower your blood alcohol concentration is:
choices: To wait and let time pass
To drink coffee
To take a cold shower
To eat a large meal
questions.explanation: Only time allows your body to process alcohol; coffee, food, and showers do not speed it up.

questions.prompt: If you plan to drink alcohol, the safest choice is to:
choices: Arrange a sober driver or other transportation in advance
Drive slowly and carefully
Wait one hour then drive
Drink only beer
questions.explanation: Planning a sober ride ahead of time guarantees you will not have to drive impaired.

questions.prompt: In Montana, you must turn on your headlights:
choices: From a half hour after sunset to a half hour before sunrise and when visibility is reduced
Only on the interstate
Only when it is fully dark
Only when other cars have theirs on
questions.explanation: Montana requires headlights from a half hour after sunset until a half hour before sunrise and whenever conditions limit visibility.

questions.prompt: In fog, rain, or snow you should use:
choices: Low-beam headlights
High-beam headlights
Hazard lights while moving
No lights to avoid glare
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog, rain, and snow and reduce visibility, so use low beams in those conditions.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
Brake hard and hold the wheel still
Accelerate to regain control
Close your eyes and brace
questions.explanation: Easing off the accelerator and steering where you want the front to go helps you recover from a skid.

questions.prompt: Which part of the road is most likely to freeze first in cold weather?
choices: Bridges and shaded areas
The center of a sunny straightaway
Steep uphill grades only
Gravel shoulders
questions.explanation: Bridges and shaded spots lose heat from both sides and freeze before other parts of the road.

questions.prompt: Hydroplaning occurs when:
choices: Tires ride up on a film of water and lose contact with the road
Brakes overheat on a long hill
The engine stalls in deep snow
Headlights fog up in rain
questions.explanation: At higher speeds on wet roads, tires can lose grip and skim on water, so slow down in the rain.

questions.prompt: Before driving in winter weather, you should:
choices: Clear snow and ice from all windows and lights
Clear only the driver's side window
Leave frost to melt while driving
Warm up by driving fast
questions.explanation: Clearing all glass and lights gives you full visibility and makes your vehicle visible to others.

questions.prompt: When driving on snow or ice, you should:
choices: Slow down and make gentle, gradual movements
Brake and steer sharply
Maintain your normal dry-road speed
Follow closely to draft other vehicles
questions.explanation: Slow speeds and smooth inputs keep tires from losing grip on slippery surfaces.

questions.prompt: If you are caught in dense fog and cannot see well, the safest action is to:
choices: Slow down and, if needed, pull completely off the road and turn on hazards
Speed up to get through it
Use high beams
Stop in the travel lane
questions.explanation: In dense fog, reduce speed and, if visibility is too poor, pull fully off the road and signal with your hazards.

questions.prompt: You may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
choices: 15 feet
5 feet
25 feet
50 feet
questions.explanation: Parking is prohibited within 15 feet of a fire hydrant so firefighters can reach it in an emergency.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb when facing downhill keeps the vehicle from rolling into traffic if it moves.

questions.prompt: In Montana, a child under 6 years old who weighs less than 60 pounds must:
choices: Be secured in an approved child safety seat
Sit in the front seat
Use only a lap belt
Be held by an adult
questions.explanation: Montana law requires children under 6 and under 60 pounds to ride in an approved child safety seat.

questions.prompt: Where is parking generally prohibited?
choices: On a crosswalk or in front of a driveway
In a marked parking lot
Along an unmarked rural shoulder where allowed
In a metered space
questions.explanation: Blocking a crosswalk or driveway is prohibited because it endangers pedestrians and blocks access.

questions.prompt: Who is required to wear a seat belt in a moving vehicle in Montana?
choices: The driver and all passengers
Only the driver
Only front-seat occupants
Only children
questions.explanation: Montana law requires the driver and every passenger to be properly buckled.

questions.prompt: When you park your vehicle, you should always:
choices: Set the parking brake and leave it in park or in gear
Leave the engine running
Leave it in neutral
Leave the keys in the ignition unlocked
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake and leaving the car in park or gear prevents it from rolling away.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Away from the curb
Toward the curb
Straight
Sharply left
questions.explanation: Facing uphill with a curb, turn the wheels away from the curb so the tire will catch the curb if the car rolls back.

questions.prompt: A curb painted a special color or a posted sign reserving a space for persons with disabilities means you:
choices: May not park there without a valid disability permit
May park there for short stops
May park there if no spaces remain
May park there at night
questions.explanation: Accessible parking spaces are reserved and require a valid disability permit or plate.

questions.prompt: In Montana, a teen may obtain a learner license beginning at what age through an approved traffic education program?
questions.explanation: Montana allows a learner license at age 14 1/2 for teens enrolled in an approved traffic education program.

questions.prompt: While driving with a Montana learner license, you must:
choices: Always be supervised by a licensed adult seated beside you
Drive alone after dark
Carry no passengers ever
Drive only on the interstate
questions.explanation: A learner license requires a licensed adult in the front passenger seat at all times.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving practice must a Montana teen complete before the next licensing stage?
choices: 50 hours, including 10 at night
20 hours total
100 hours total
30 hours, all in daylight
questions.explanation: Montana's GDL program requires 50 hours of supervised practice, with at least 10 of them at night.

questions.prompt: How long must a teen hold a Montana learner license before moving to a first-year restricted license?
choices: At least 6 months
At least 1 month
At least 2 years
No minimum time
questions.explanation: The learner license must be held for a minimum of 6 months before advancing in the GDL program.

questions.prompt: Under Montana's first-year restricted license, nighttime driving is generally restricted between:
choices: Midnight and 4:00 a.m.
questions.explanation: Restricted teen drivers may not drive between 11:00 p.m. and 5:00 a.m. except for certain allowed purposes.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of Montana's Graduated Driver Licensing (GDL) program?
choices: To introduce new drivers to driving gradually with supervision and restrictions
To make licenses more expensive
To limit driving to adults only
To replace the written test
questions.explanation: GDL builds experience in stages so new drivers gain skill under reduced-risk conditions.

questions.prompt: When driving, you should carry which of the following?
choices: Your driver license, registration, and proof of insurance
Only your license
Only proof of insurance
Just your registration
questions.explanation: You must be able to show your license, vehicle registration, and proof of insurance when requested.

questions.prompt: If you move within Montana, you should update the address on your driver license:
choices: Within the time required by law
Only when it expires
Never, it is optional
Only if you change vehicles
questions.explanation: Drivers must keep their license address current so official notices reach them.

questions.prompt: If you are stopped by a law enforcement officer, you should:
choices: Pull over safely to the right, stay in your vehicle, and keep your hands visible
Get out and walk to the officer
Keep driving to a parking lot miles away
Reach into the glovebox before the officer asks
questions.explanation: Pulling over safely and keeping your hands visible makes the stop safer for you and the officer.

questions.prompt: A DUI conviction in Montana can result in:
choices: Fines, license suspension, and possible jail time
A verbal warning only
No penalty for a first offense
Loss of registration only
questions.explanation: Montana penalizes DUI with fines, suspension of driving privileges, and potential jail.

questions.prompt: Speeding in a posted school zone or work zone typically results in:
choices: Higher fines than usual
A reduced fine
No penalty
A free warning each time
questions.explanation: Penalties are increased in school and work zones to protect children and roadside workers.

questions.prompt: Driving while your license is suspended will result in:
choices: Additional penalties and possible extension of the suspension
No further consequences
Automatic reinstatement
A reduced insurance rate
questions.explanation: Driving on a suspended license adds new penalties and can lengthen the suspension.

questions.prompt: Failing to obey Montana's Move Over law for stopped emergency vehicles can lead to:
choices: Significant fines
A thank-you note
No consequence
A discount on registration
questions.explanation: Violating the Move Over law endangers roadside workers and carries substantial penalties.

questions.prompt: Operating a vehicle without the required insurance in Montana can result in:
choices: Fines and other penalties
No penalty
A warning only
A reduced license fee
questions.explanation: Montana requires proof of insurance, and driving without it brings fines and other penalties.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash that causes injury or significant property damage, you must:
choices: Stop, render aid if needed, and report it as required
Leave the scene quickly
Wait only if you caused it
Report it only if asked later
questions.explanation: The law requires you to stop, help the injured, exchange information, and report the crash when required.

questions.prompt: Accumulating too many traffic violations can lead to:
choices: Suspension of your driving privileges
A reward from the state
Lower insurance rates
An automatic license upgrade
questions.explanation: Repeated violations can add up and result in suspension of your license.
